claimText: Under the Income Tax Act 2025, Minimum Alternate Tax (MAT) applies exclusively to corporate taxpayers, whereas Alternate Minimum Tax (AMT) applies to non-corporate taxpayers.
evidenceExcerpt: The provisions of MAT are applicable to a corporate taxpayer only. AMT has similar provisions as MAT which is applicable to non-corporate tax payers.
examUse: Statement-based question swapping the applicability of MAT and AMT.
distractorHooks: Both MAT and AMT apply to all taxpayers

claimText: The definition of undisclosed income under the Income Tax Act 2025 has been expanded to include virtual digital assets.
evidenceExcerpt: The definition of undisclosed income... is expanded to include virtual digital assets.
examUse: Statement-based question on the scope of undisclosed income in search cases.
distractorHooks: Virtual digital assets are explicitly excluded from the definition of undisclosed income
Undisclosed income only covers physical assets and fiat currency

claimText: Income tax authorities have the power to access virtual digital spaces, including email servers and social media, during search and seizure proceedings.
evidenceExcerpt: Income tax authorities can access virtual digital spaces (e.g., email servers, social media) during search and seizure proceedings.
examUse: Statement-based question regarding the extent of powers granted to tax authorities during search and seizure.
distractorHooks: Tax authorities require a separate judicial warrant to access social media
Search and seizure powers are restricted to physical premises and documents

claimText: A dispute resolution panel is available for eligible assessees, which includes entities involved in transfer pricing cases, non-residents, and foreign companies.
evidenceExcerpt: A dispute resolution panel is available for eligible assessees, including those in transfer pricing cases, non-residents, and foreign companies.
examUse: Statement-based question testing the eligibility criteria for accessing the dispute resolution panel.
distractorHooks: The dispute resolution panel is exclusively for resident Indian citizens
Foreign companies are barred from accessing the dispute resolution panel
